name: nklisch-skills
description: Native agent plugins and portable skills maintained by nklisch.

plugins.name: workflow
plugins.description: Software development workflow skills — ideate projects, design features, implement from plans, profile and optimize performance, refactor codebases with bold architectural reconceptions, extract patterns, design tests, and ship releases.

plugins.name: nates-toolkit
plugins.description: Standalone, project-agnostic utility skills with no workflow lock-in — plainspeak (plain-language re-explanation), agent-reflection (self-reflection on tool & skill usage), write-tool-skill + skill-auditor (skill authoring and quality auditing).

plugins.name: code-audit
plugins.description: Standalone markdown-first code audit skills — deep code scan, bug scan, security scan, test scan, perf scout, bold refactor, and repo eval without requiring agile-workflow or .work.

plugins.name: agile-workflow
plugins.description: Markdown-based work-tracking substrate for AI-driven projects. Items as files in .work/, late-binding releases, gates that produce items, goal-backed autopilot queue runner.

plugins.name: workbench
plugins.description: Requirements-first software delivery with a lightweight .work/ ledger, integrated research and interactive UI walkthroughs, judgment-led execution, and compact release summaries.

plugins.name: ux-ui-design
plugins.description: HTML/CSS/JS mockup-first UI/UX design. Generate multi-option screen mockups, multi-page user flows, and design-system palettes as throwaway single-file mockups in .mockups/ for alignment before implementation. Loosely integrates with agile-workflow.

plugins.name: agentic-research
plugins.description: Agentic Research Discipline (ARD) — grounded, verifiable AI research with a non-erodable anti-fabrication floor, selectable verification gates, and a .research/ substrate tier paralleling .work/. ARD is the plugin's internal, empirically-warranted discipline (ard-core/).

plugins.name: agent-coordination
plugins.description: Sparse cross-agent coordination ledger for shared repositories. Uses deliberate GitHub Discussion events for claims, handoffs, blockers, review summaries, and merge summaries without turning Discussions into chat.

plugins.name: zai-research
plugins.description: Z.ai-powered web search, URL/PDF reading, and GitHub repo deep-read for Pi. Wraps Z.ai's web-search-prime, web-reader, and zread MCP servers via the bundled MCP SDK client (pi has no built-in MCP), plus provider-free local PDF extraction via unpdf. Auth is the configured zai provider key. Replaces the prior pi-web-access web-search/fetch surface.

plugins.name: krometrail
plugins.description: Control a local Chromium browser and inspect transient behavior with source-linked temporal visual evidence.

plugins.name: peeragent
plugins.description: Delegate arbitrary task work between local coding agents — any host (Claude Code, Codex, or Pi) can call any peer (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini through Antigravity, or Z.AI GLM 5.2 through Pi). Ships the peer and peer-review skills.

plugins.name: skilltap
plugins.description: Personal control plane for Codex and Claude Code environments — adopt, reconcile, update, and bootstrap native plugins, complete skills, and shared instructions without marketplace discovery.
